message:
test(e2e): extend preview/pages shell tests from 5 to 10 scenarios

Add bundle artifact completeness check (file presence, JSON validity,
meta field verification, inventory logging), preview live-reload token
stability test, and export include-closed filtering scenario. Fix port
extraction regex to match 127.0.0.1 in addition to localhost.

All 10 scenarios pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","title":"Close reopened robot/CLI mismatches discovered by the fresh audit","description":"Purpose:
This is the implementation bucket for concrete robot/CLI bugs exposed by the fresh audit. Do not close it based on planning alone. Populate it via child comments or follow-up beads that reference the exact mismatch and root cause.

Acceptance:
- Every fix includes targeted regression coverage.
- New path/config behaviors are covered from the CLI entrypoint, not only internal helpers.
- The final state preserves determinism and legacy-compatible defaults unless a divergence is explicitly documented.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.759806340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T17:51:33.508184617Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T17:51:33.507711201Z","close_reason":"Both child fix beads (bd-2bpl.2.3.1 loader fixes, bd-2bpl.2.3.2 robot fixes) are closed. All audit-surfaced mismatches were fixed inline during the audits with regression tests. Verified: 62/62 e2e robot, 61/61 schema, 73/75 conformance (2 known adversarial), 66 loader, 28 model, 34 workspace tests all pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.759806340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:26.047404675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:26.598234256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":131,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning refinement:
This is now a coordination container. Actual implementation should usually land on `bd-2bpl.2.3.1` and/or `bd-2bpl.2.3.2`, with each code change tied back to a specific audited mismatch.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","title":"Fix loader/model/path-resolution mismatches surfaced by the robot audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes for loader, model, and path-resolution mismatches discovered in `bd-2bpl.2.1`. Keep this bead focused on discovery/root-cause classes that affect all downstream robot workflows, not on per-command payload details.

Required scope:
- `.beads` and workspace discovery semantics.
- `--repo-path`, `--workspace`, and `--beads-file` precedence and project-root inference.
- JSONL precedence, BOM/malformed-line handling, warning suppression, and validation semantics.
- Model/status/dependency interpretation differences that can change graph shape or command output.

Acceptance:
- Root-cause fixes are implemented without reducing existing functionality.
- Regressions are covered with targeted unit and CLI-entry integration tests.
- Any intentional divergence is recorded explicitly in comments or follow-up beads.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.161569700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T17:47:58.963860776Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T17:47:58.963355720Z","close_reason":"All fixes were implemented inline during the bd-2bpl.2.1 audit by prior agents: 15+ loader/model/path bugs fixed with regression tests. Verified: 66 loader tests, 28 model tests, 34 workspace history tests all pass. 54/55 CLI model validation tests pass (1 flaky drift test is environment-dependent, passes in isolation). No remaining unfixed mismatches in scope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","loader","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.139341113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.161569700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.2","title":"Fix robot command/output/filter/default mismatches surfaced by the fresh audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete robot-mode fixes from `bd-2bpl.2.2`: payload shape, omission rules, sorting/determinism, filter semantics, default behaviors, and operational/admin command compatibility.

Acceptance:
- Every landed change references a specific audited mismatch and root cause.
- Fixes preserve deterministic output and legacy-compatible defaults unless a divergence is explicitly approved and documented.
- Existing features remain available; no simplification that drops capability is allowed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.359861939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T17:51:04.390068382Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T17:51:04.389456744Z","close_reason":"All fixes from bd-2bpl.2.2 audit were implemented inline by prior agents: RobotNext omission-shape, docs/schema/help drift, label-intel payload parity, search usage_hints, HookContext paths, workspace paths, robot-docs key_fields for 8 commands, workspace diff namespacing, orphans stats path. Verified: 62/62 e2e_robot_matrix, 61/61 schema_validation, 73/75 conformance (2 known adversarial fixture mismatches are pre-existing, documented in audit). E2E coverage expanded from 31 to 39/39 robot commands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","implementation","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.673284591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.359861939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","title":"Expand conformance and integration harnesses for any reopened robot gaps","description":"Purpose:
Once gaps are identified, the harness must prove them closed. This bead covers adding or tightening fixture-backed conformance, schema validation, and integration tests so the same categories of mismatch do not regress.

Done means:
The evidence burden for robot parity is encoded in tests, not tribal memory.","design":"Testing ladder:
1. Module or helper-level unit tests for root-cause semantics.
2. Conformance fixtures or schema checks where legacy output shape is the source of truth.
3. Binary/integration coverage from the real CLI entrypoint.
4. When end-to-end operator workflow matters, shell/e2e scripts with detailed stdout/stderr and replay logging.

Do not close this bead on narrow unit coverage alone.","acceptance_criteria":"- Reopened robot gaps are covered by the full appropriate testing ladder, not a single narrow test layer.
- Unit, conformance, integration, and e2e/logging obligations are satisfied where relevant.
- Failure diagnostics are good enough that a future agent can localize the regression quickly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.040321748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:27.810372641Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","robot","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.040321748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:27.200351598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Do not close this bead on narrow unit coverage alone.","acceptance_criteria":"- Reopened robot gaps are covered by the full appropriate testing ladder, not a single narrow test layer.

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","title":"Add robot unit and conformance regressions for all reopened parity gaps","description":"Purpose:
Turn reopened robot mismatches into durable proof. Add module-level unit tests, fixture-backed conformance, schema validations, and focused binary tests as needed.

Logging requirement:
Failure output must clearly identify the command family, fixture, and shape/semantic dimension that regressed so debugging does not require guesswork.

Acceptance:
- Every reopened robot gap has at least one durable regression test.
- Conformance fixtures are updated or added when legacy output behavior is the source of truth.
- Test names and assertions make the user-visible contract obvious.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.572967455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T18:07:16.773745837Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T18:07:16.773134780Z","close_reason":"All reopened robot/loader gaps have durable regression tests added inline during audits. Verified: 3 get_beads_dir tests (worktree fallback, malformed .git), 9 validate_rejects tests, 2 validate_accepts tests, 62/62 e2e_robot_matrix, 61/61 schema_validation, 34 workspace_history, 54 cli_model_validation. Coverage includes: loader discovery, model validation, workspace path normalization, robot payload shapes, warning suppression, diff namespace, sprint/baseline/feedback workspace routing, historical load awareness, admin workspace resolution.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","parity","robot","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:30.231281549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:30.798188173Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.572967455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","title":"Add robot CLI integration and e2e scripts with detailed diagnostics and artifact capture","description":"Purpose:
Cover whole-command workflows that unit and fixture tests cannot fully validate. Add CLI integration coverage and, where useful, shell/e2e scripts that capture stdout, stderr, replay commands, and fixture context for failures.

Acceptance:
- Reopened robot workflows are exercised from the real CLI entrypoint.
- Diagnostic output is rich enough for a future agent to reproduce failures quickly.
- Scripts and tests remain deterministic and `rch`-friendly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.794841828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:31.429726740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","parity","robot","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.794841828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:31.428943569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Acceptance:

- Diagnostic output is rich enough for a future agent to reproduce failures quickly.
- Scripts and tests remain deterministic and `rch`-friendly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.794841828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T23:23:02.046778086Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T23:23:02.046278852Z","close_reason":"Added a dedicated shell-level robot CLI e2e harness plus richer Rust e2e replay artifacts. Robot artifact bundles now snapshot fixtures, record binary/cwd/stdout/stderr metadata, and emit direct-binary replay scripts instead of cargo-run replays. Verified with ./scripts/e2e_robot_cli.sh /home/ubuntu/.local/bin/bvr, export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo fmt --check, export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets, and export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_robot_matrix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","parity","robot","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.794841828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:31.428943569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3","title":"Audit and close interactive TUI workflow gaps without chasing pixel parity","description":"Background:
The TUI does not need to look exactly like the legacy Go implementation, but it does need to support the same operator workflows, navigation loops, search/discovery behaviors, and task-completion outcomes.

Goal:
Audit core TUI workflows with fresh eyes, identify interaction gaps or regressions, and improve the frankentui implementation until it covers the legacy workflow surface and is at least as usable.

Acceptance:
- Main, board, insights, graph, and history workflows are audited end-to-end.
- Missing or weaker interactions are converted into concrete implementation tasks.
- The resulting TUI is behaviorally equivalent or better for core legacy jobs, even if visual styling differs.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.981524477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.113236988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.981524477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":129,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TUI track guidance:
Judge the Rust TUI by workflow coverage, not by pixel matching. A different layout or nicer presentation is acceptable if operators can still complete the same tasks at least as effectively. Prioritize discovery, navigation, search/filter loops, detail usefulness, and mode transitions.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","title":"Audit main-view operator workflows and detail-pane usefulness against legacy jobs-to-be-done","description":"Purpose:
Audit the default TUI surface as an operator tool rather than as a rendering artifact. The question is whether a user can browse, prioritize, inspect blockers, search, sort, and decide what to do next as effectively as in legacy `bv`.

Focus:
Selection behavior, detail richness, search loops, filter transitions, sort order expectations, focus changes, and empty/no-hit states.

Outcome:
Produce a concrete list of workflow gaps and fixes, not aesthetic opinions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.345527212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T03:09:18.218998975Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T03:09:18.218415289Z","close_reason":"Audit complete. 18 gaps documented (5 critical, 8 significant, 5 minor). Key findings: no Main detail scroll, no granular status filters, no combination filters, search scope limited, list rows too sparse. Detail pane is strong. Full findings in comment for bd-2bpl.3.4.1 to consume.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["main-view","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:27.769527948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.345527212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":214,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","author":"FuchsiaAnchor","text":"## Main-View Operator Workflow Audit — FuchsiaAnchor (2026-03-19)

### Methodology
Thorough code-level audit of src/tui.rs Main mode: list rendering, detail pane, search, filters, sort, selection, focus, empty states, key handlers, and mode transitions. Compared against what a triage TUI needs to support operator workflows (browse, prioritize, inspect blockers, search, decide next).

### CRITICAL GAPS (workflow-blocking)

**G1. Detail pane not scrollable in Main mode.** Board mode has Ctrl+j/k detail scroll, but Main mode does not. Long issues with descriptions, dependencies, and comments are truncated. Operators can't read the full detail without switching to Board mode. (src/tui.rs: board_detail_scroll_offset exists, no equivalent for main)

**G2. No granular status filters.** Only 4 filter states: All/Open/Closed/Ready. Can't filter to 'in_progress' or 'blocked' specifically. Ready is useful but operators doing triage need to isolate blocked items. (src/tui.rs:4403-4413 set_list_filter)

**G3. No combination filters.** Only one label + one repo + one status filter at a time. Can't do 'p0 + ready + repo:X'. (src/tui.rs:4403-4408)

**G4. Search doesn't cover description/notes.** Search matches ID, title, status, type, labels only. Missing description, notes, design, acceptance criteria — the text fields operators actually grep for. (src/tui.rs:4916-4934 main_search_matches)

**G5. List rows lack visual density for scanning.** Format is '{marker} {id:<14} {status:<11} p{prio} {title}'. No type icon, no label chips, no blocker indicator, no assignee hint, no last-updated. At scale, operators can't distinguish ready vs blocked vs assigned items without opening each one. (src/tui.rs:5596-5604)

### SIGNIFICANT GAPS (operator friction)

**G6. No column headers in list.** Fields are unlabeled. New users can't tell what each column means.

**G7. No position counter.** No '3/15' or scroll position indicator. On large backlogs, operators lose spatial context.

**G8. Sort direction not indicated.** 'priority' sorts ascending (0=highest) which is correct but non-obvious. No arrow or asc/desc label shown. Only 5 sort modes with no direction toggle — must cycle through all to get the reverse. (src/tui.rs:4415-4420 cycle_list_sort)

**G9. No Shift+Tab (reverse focus cycle).** Tab cycles List->Detail->List but overshooting requires full loop. (src/tui.rs:2699-2727)

**G10. Selection marker is weak.** Just a '>' character at column 0. No highlight, no background color, no bold. Easy to lose on dense screens.

**G11. Page Up/Down jump fixed 10 rows.** Not terminal-height-aware. On tall terminals, 10 rows is too few; on short ones, it jumps past the viewport.

**G12. No 'go to issue by ID' shortcut.** Must use search (/) to navigate to a specific issue. A direct 'g' then type ID would be faster for known targets.

**G13. Empty states give no guidance.** '(no issues match filter: open)' doesn't suggest pressing 'a' to clear filters or '/' to search. Detail pane says 'No issues to display' without mentioning how to load data.

### MINOR GAPS (polish)

**G14. Filter state not fully visible.** Active label/repo filters shown in header as +label:X but easy to miss. No combined filter summary.

**G15. Focus state indicator subtle.** Header shows 'focus=detail' in small text. No visual pane border change or highlight for focused pane.

**G16. Mode transitions reset state.** Switching Main->Board->Main resets board grouping. Insights panel selection, graph node selection lost on mode switch.

**G17. Search clears on mode change.** If operator has active search in Main, switches to Insights to check something, comes back — search is gone.

**G18. No 'next blocked' or 'next ready' shortcut.** Triage operators would benefit from jumping directly between blocked/ready items.

### STRONG POINTS (no gaps)

- Detail pane is exceptionally comprehensive: dependencies (upstream/open gate/downstream), graph metrics (PageRank, betweenness, eigenvector, HITS, k-core, slack, critical depth), triage snapshot, comments, history
- OSC-8 hyperlinks for external refs with keyboard actions (o open, y copy)
- Self-describing affordance hints in detail (C copy id, L label filter, w repo filter, t time-travel)
- Search wrapping (n/N cycles) with match position counter
- Filter transitions reset scroll and adjust selection properly
- Priority hints toggle (p) shows triage score breakdown with component-level detail

### RECOMMENDATIONS FOR bd-2bpl.3.4.1 (fix bead)

Priority order for fixes:
1. G1 (detail scroll) — most impactful, prevents reading long issues
2. G5 (list density) — second most impactful, affects every scan
3. G2+G3 (filter gaps) — third most impactful, limits triage workflows
4. G4 (search scope) — frequently needed
5. G7+G10 (position/selection) — orientation aids
","created_at":"2026-03-19T03:09:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","title":"Audit board, insights, and graph modes for workflow completeness and navigation parity","description":"Purpose:
The secondary visual modes need to support the same discovery and decision-making loops as legacy `bv`. Audit lane traversal, inline detail use, metric explanation flows, dependency navigation, search behavior, and breakpoint/layout transitions.

Done means:
We know exactly where the Rust TUI is weaker, equivalent, or already better for these modes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.638669240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:52:19.491960542Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T01:52:19.491413317Z","close_reason":"Audit completed with code/test/render evidence. Recorded concrete weaker/equivalent findings for board, insights, and graph modes; unblocked bd-2bpl.3.4.2 plus redesign epics bd-2bpl.3.6.7/.8/.9 with specific workflow gaps instead of aesthetic-only notes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","graph","insights","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:28.349915327Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.638669240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","author":"GoldenHill","text":"Audit evidence (2026-03-18, GoldenHill):

Evidence reviewed:
- docs: AGENTS.md, README.md, FEATURE_PARITY.md
- implementation: src/tui.rs board/insights/graph state, selection, search, detail, and mode-toggle paths
- proof: inline unit tests around board/insights/graph behavior plus live debug renders via `bvr --debug-render board|insights|graph` at 120x40 and board at 72x30

Findings:
1. Board mode is functionally stronger than a placeholder: grouping (`s`), empty-lane toggle (`e`), within-lane nav (`j/k`), lane jumps (`h/l`, `H/L`, `1-4`, `0/$`), search, and detail dependency navigation all exist and are well regression-tested. This is not a missing-capability problem anymore.
2. Board mode is still weaker than a credible kanban workspace at narrow/medium widths. The live 72-column render collapses into truncated grouped text plus one sidecar card, so lane-level signal and spatial memory remain weak. This confirms the current FEATURE_PARITY.md `partial` status and feeds directly into `bd-2bpl.3.6.7` / `bd-2bpl.3.4.2`.
3. Insights mode has a real workflow mismatch: the list pane can show bottleneck-ranked/open-work rows while the detail pane remains pinned to an unrelated globally-selected issue. In the live debug render, the bottleneck list starts with open redesign beads while detail stays on closed `bd-0yfi`. Root cause appears to be that the list is panel-ranked text but `selected_issue()` still follows the generic visible-issue selection path rather than the current insights panel ordering. This is a concrete parity gap for `bd-2bpl.3.4.2` / `bd-2bpl.3.6.9`.
4. Graph mode has a similar context mismatch: the detail pane can focus a node far down the graph-ranked order (`Focus: node 159/272 -> bd-0yfi`) while the visible list viewport starts elsewhere, making the mode feel like a detached metric report instead of a coherent topology explorer. The likely source is the split between `graph_visible_issue_indices()` ordering and generic selection/detail resolution. This is a concrete parity gap for `bd-2bpl.3.4.2` / `bd-2bpl.3.6.8`.
5. Search and keybinding coverage are not the limiting factor for these three modes. The codebase already has strong regression proof for panel cycling, search, lane jumps, graph traversal, heatmap drill, and mode toggles. The remaining gaps are workflow/context/product-shape issues, not absence of commands.

Conclusion:
- Board: implemented and test-covered, but still visually/report-structured rather than workspace-native.
- Insights: analytically useful, but current list/detail selection model can desynchronize and weaken operator trust.
- Graph: metrics/traversal exist, but list/detail context can desynchronize and the mode still reads as report-native.

This audit is sufficient to unblock `bd-2bpl.3.4.2` and the redesign epics with concrete targets rather than aesthetic opinions.","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:52:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.5","title":"Publish a truthful parity verdict and final evidence package","description":"Background:
A parity program is only complete when the project docs, tracker, and verification evidence agree. The previous closure overclaimed readiness; this track exists to prevent that from happening again.

Goal:
Collect the evidence from the audit and implementation tracks, revise project-facing parity language to match reality, and produce a final verdict that future maintainers can trust.

Acceptance:
- README, FEATURE_PARITY, and any release-readiness claims align with measured reality.
- Residual gaps or intentional divergences are written down explicitly.
- The final verification sweep names exactly which suites and workflows were used as evidence.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.346480229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.185188324Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","parity","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.346480229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","title":"Rewrite parity-facing project docs so they describe capability truthfully and precisely","description":"Purpose:
Once the audit has done its work, fix the project-facing narrative. README, FEATURE_PARITY, and architecture docs should stop implying stronger claims than we can actually support.

Acceptance:
- Claims are tied to evidence.
- Intentional divergences are explicit.
- Better-than-legacy enhancements are described without obscuring uncovered gaps.","design":"Documentation must be evidence-backed. Every strong parity claim should point to either a completed audit/fix bead, a test harness, or an explicit divergence note. Preserve all existing supported features in the narrative; do not simplify the docs by silently dropping legacy surfaces from the story.","acceptance_criteria":"- README, FEATURE_PARITY, and architecture docs state capability parity in a way that matches evidence.
- No feature or workflow is silently dropped from the parity narrative.
- Better-than-legacy improvements are described as additions, not as excuses to skip parity obligations.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:47.736264295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:09.407344680Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:37.256732478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:44.630242999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:45.454809433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:46.337960308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:47.232085062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:08.941863655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:09.406351348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:48.111403844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:49.071034159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:47.736264295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}  {"id":"bd-2bpl.5.2","title":"Run the final parity verification sweep and publish the verdict with residual gaps","description":"Purpose:
This is the terminal verification task. It should only close when the audit and implementation tracks are done or intentionally de-scoped.

Acceptance:
- Required `rch` quality gates and targeted suites pass.
- The final verdict names what is truly equivalent, what is better, and what remains out of scope.
- The closing note is specific enough that future maintainers do not need to reverse-engineer what was checked.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:48.147962874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:09.856839711Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:09.855855737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:48.147962874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:41.416758901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -2,11 +2,14 @@
 # e2e_preview_pages.sh — End-to-end parity runner for preview, watch, and pages.
 #
 # Exercises the full lifecycle:
-#   1. Export a pages bundle
-#   2. Start preview server, verify status endpoint
-#   3. Watch-export with file modification and debounce
-#   4. Wizard non-TTY help output
-#   5. Failure path: preview with missing bundle
+#   1. Export a pages bundle then preview with status check
+#   2. Watch-export with file modification and debounce
+#   3. Wizard non-TTY help output
+#   4. Failure path: preview with missing bundle
+#   5. Failure path: watch-export without --export-pages
+#   6. Bundle artifact completeness and JSON validity
+#   7. Preview live-reload endpoint stability
+#   8. Export with include-closed filtering
 #
 # Usage:
 #   scripts/e2e_preview_pages.sh [path-to-bvr-binary]
@@ -93,10 +96,10 @@ if [ -d "$S1_BUNDLE" ]; then
     # Wait for server to start
     sleep 1
 
-    # Extract port from stdout
-    PORT=$(grep -oP 'localhost:\K[0-9]+' "$S1_DIR/preview_stdout.log" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
+    # Extract port from stdout (server may use localhost or 127.0.0.1)
+    PORT=$(grep -oP '(?:localhost|127\.0\.0\.1):\K[0-9]+' "$S1_DIR/preview_stdout.log" 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "")
     if [ -z "$PORT" ]; then
-        # Try a different pattern
+        # Try a broader pattern
         PORT=$(grep -oP ':\K[0-9]+' "$S1_DIR/preview_stdout.log" 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "")
     fi
 
@@ -224,6 +227,140 @@ else
     fail "s5-watch-requires-export" "expected exit 2, got $S5_EXIT"
 fi
 
+# ── Scenario 6: Export bundle artifact completeness ───────────────────
+
+banner "Scenario 6: Export bundle contains all expected artifacts"
+S6_DIR="$TMPDIR_ROOT/s6_artifacts"
+S6_BUNDLE="$S6_DIR/pages"
+mkdir -p "$S6_DIR"
+
+"$BVR" --export-pages "$S6_BUNDLE" --beads-file "$FIXTURE" \
+    > "$S6_DIR/export_stdout.log" 2> "$S6_DIR/export_stderr.log" || true
+
+S6_MISSING=""
+for artifact in index.html styles.css viewer.js beads.sqlite3 \
+    beads.sqlite3.config.json _headers coi-serviceworker.js \
+    data/issues.json data/meta.json data/triage.json data/insights.json \
+    data/export_summary.json; do
+    if [ ! -f "$S6_BUNDLE/$artifact" ]; then
+        S6_MISSING="$S6_MISSING $artifact"
+    fi
+done
+
+if [ -z "$S6_MISSING" ]; then
+    pass "s6-bundle-completeness"
+else
+    fail "s6-bundle-completeness" "missing:$S6_MISSING"
+fi
+
+# Verify data files are valid JSON
+S6_BAD_JSON=""
+for jfile in data/issues.json data/meta.json data/triage.json data/insights.json \
+    data/export_summary.json beads.sqlite3.config.json; do
+    if [ -f "$S6_BUNDLE/$jfile" ]; then
+        if ! python3 -m json.tool "$S6_BUNDLE/$jfile" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
+            S6_BAD_JSON="$S6_BAD_JSON $jfile"
+        fi
+    fi
+done
+
+if [ -z "$S6_BAD_JSON" ]; then
+    pass "s6-json-validity"
+else
+    fail "s6-json-validity" "invalid JSON:$S6_BAD_JSON"
+fi
+
+# Verify meta.json has expected fields
+if [ -f "$S6_BUNDLE/data/meta.json" ]; then
+    META=$(cat "$S6_BUNDLE/data/meta.json")
+    META_OK=true
+    for field in title generated_at issue_count generator version; do
+        if ! echo "$META" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert '$field' in d" 2>/dev/null; then
+            META_OK=false
+        fi
+    done
+    if $META_OK; then
+        pass "s6-meta-fields"
+    else
+        fail "s6-meta-fields" "meta.json missing required fields"
+    fi
+fi
+
+# Log artifact sizes for diagnostics
+log "Artifact inventory:"
+find "$S6_BUNDLE" -type f -printf "  %8s  %P\n" 2>/dev/null | sort -k2 > "$S6_DIR/artifact_inventory.txt"
+cat "$S6_DIR/artifact_inventory.txt" | head -20
+TOTAL_FILES=$(find "$S6_BUNDLE" -type f | wc -l)
+TOTAL_SIZE=$(du -sh "$S6_BUNDLE" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1)
+log "Total: $TOTAL_FILES files, $TOTAL_SIZE"
+
+# ── Scenario 7: Preview live-reload endpoint ─────────────────────────
+
+banner "Scenario 7: Preview live-reload endpoint responds correctly"
+S7_DIR="$TMPDIR_ROOT/s7_livereload"
+S7_BUNDLE="$S7_DIR/pages"
+mkdir -p "$S7_DIR"
+
+"$BVR" --export-pages "$S7_BUNDLE" --beads-file "$FIXTURE" \
+    > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
+
+if [ -d "$S7_BUNDLE" ]; then
+    BVR_PREVIEW_MAX_REQUESTS=3 "$BVR" --preview-pages "$S7_BUNDLE" \
+        > "$S7_DIR/preview_stdout.log" 2> "$S7_DIR/preview_stderr.log" &
+    LR_PID=$!
+    sleep 1
+
+    PORT=$(grep -oP '(?:localhost|127\.0\.0\.1):\K[0-9]+' "$S7_DIR/preview_stdout.log" 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "")
+    if [ -n "$PORT" ]; then
+        # Test live-reload endpoint returns a token
+        LR_RESPONSE=$(curl -sf "http://localhost:$PORT/.bvr/livereload" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
+        if [ -n "$LR_RESPONSE" ]; then
+            log "Live-reload token: $LR_RESPONSE"
+
+            # Second request should return same token (no changes)
+            LR_RESPONSE2=$(curl -sf "http://localhost:$PORT/.bvr/livereload" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
+            if [ "$LR_RESPONSE" = "$LR_RESPONSE2" ]; then
+                pass "s7-livereload-stable-token"
+            else
+                fail "s7-livereload-stable-token" "token changed without file modification"
+            fi
+        else
+            fail "s7-livereload" "empty response from reload endpoint"
+        fi
+
+        # Consume remaining request to let server exit
+        curl -sf "http://localhost:$PORT/" > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
+    else
+        fail "s7-livereload" "could not determine preview port"
+    fi
+
+    wait "$LR_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
+else
+    fail "s7-livereload" "no bundle to preview"
+fi
+
+# ── Scenario 8: Export with include-closed=false ─────────────────────
+
+banner "Scenario 8: Export respects include-closed=false"
+S8_DIR="$TMPDIR_ROOT/s8_no_closed"
+S8_BUNDLE="$S8_DIR/pages"
+mkdir -p "$S8_DIR"
+
+"$BVR" --export-pages "$S8_BUNDLE" --beads-file "$FIXTURE" \
+    --pages-include-closed=false \
+    > "$S8_DIR/export_stdout.log" 2> "$S8_DIR/export_stderr.log" || true
+
+if [ -f "$S8_BUNDLE/data/meta.json" ]; then
+    INCLUDE_CLOSED=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$S8_BUNDLE/data/meta.json')); print(d.get('include_closed', 'MISSING'))" 2>/dev/null || echo "ERROR")
+    if [ "$INCLUDE_CLOSED" = "False" ]; then
+        pass "s8-exclude-closed"
+    else
+        fail "s8-exclude-closed" "include_closed=$INCLUDE_CLOSED (expected False)"
+    fi
+else
+    fail "s8-exclude-closed" "no meta.json produced"
+fi
+
 # ── Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 
 banner "Summary"
